jobs.title: Military Advisor- Operations (Part Time/ Remote) (Mission Assurance 4)- 29178
jobs.jd_full:
Requisition Number: 29178 

Required Travel: 51 - 75%

Employment Type: Part Time/Hourly/Non-Exempt

Anticipated Salary Range: $106,124.00 - $170,000.00 

Security Clearance: Ability to Obtain

Level of Experience: Senior HI

 

This opportunity resides with All-Domain Operations (ADO), a business group within HII’s Mission Technologies division. All-Domain Operations comprises multi-domain operations, platforms and logistics, and intelligence operations.

HII designs, develops, integrates and manages the sensors, systems and other assets necessary to support integrated ISR operations and accelerated decision-making. With data fusion and mission management capabilities for the Department of Defense, the combatant commands and the intelligence community, HII advances the mission around the globe.

 

Leadership Mindset at HII – Mission Technologies

Leadership at HII is a mindset, not a title. Through our Leadership Capability Framework, we define how every team member contributes to our mission and culture:

  • Know & Grow Your People – Commit to learning and supporting team success.
  • Build Relationships – Communicate openly, collaborate well, and build trust.
  • Take Ownership – Deliver on commitments and take pride in your work.
  • Customer First – Focus on the mission and those we serve.
  • Shape the Future – Bring ideas, curiosity, and continuous improvement.
  • Act with Urgency – Take initiative and follow through with purpose.

These capabilities guide how all employees contribute to our shared success across Mission Technologies.

Summary

HII-Mission Technologies is seeking a highly skilled and experienced Military Advisor- Operations to join our team in support of critical missions within the United States Department of Defense. The ideal candidate will be a graduate of the United States Army Command and General Staff College (CGSC), with extensive leadership experience and a proven track record of success in military operations within the INDOPACIFIC Command (INDOPACOM) Area of Responsibility (AOR). The candidate should possess strong instructional capabilities and the ability to advise senior military personnel on a variety of strategic, operational, and tactical matters. Come join our growing team!

 

*Position: Remote and part time.

 

*Travel: This position requires episodic travel to OCONUS locations, often to remote and challenging environments. Expect deployments lasting several weeks to months at a time. Travel may include locations with limited infrastructure and security concerns.

What you will do

  • Provide military advisory support to U.S. and allied forces operating within the INDOPACOM AOR, assisting with mission planning, operational execution, and strategic assessments
  • Mentor and advise senior military leaders on command and staff operations, ensuring the effective use of resources, personnel, and equipment to achieve mission objectives
  • Deliver training and instruction on military doctrine, strategy, and operations to both U.S. and allied personnel
  • Collaborate with interagency and multinational teams to enhance operational coordination and effectiveness
  • Support the development and execution of military exercises, engagements, and other operational initiatives in support of U.S. national security interests
  • Conduct assessments, briefings, and after-action reviews to ensure continuous improvement and mission success
  • Maintain situational awareness of the regional security environment, providing timely insights and recommendations to senior leadership
  • Additional duties as assigned or required 

What we are looking for

  • 9 years relevant experience with Bachelors in related field; 7 years relevant experience with Masters in related field; 4 years relevant experience with PhD or Juris Doctorate in related field; or High School Diploma or equivalent and 13 years relevant experience.
  • Proven leadership experience in military operations, with a strong background in advising, mentoring, and instructing military personnel
  • Experience with the INDOPACOM AOR, with a deep understanding of the regional security landscape, operational challenges, and strategic goals
  • Instructor experience, with the ability to deliver complex military concepts to diverse audiences
  • Strong leadership, problem-solving, and decision-making skills
  • Ability to communicate effectively in high-stress environments, both in writing and verbally
  • Willingness to deploy up to 50% of the time as required by mission needs
  • Clearance: Must be able to obtain and maintain a Secret Clearance 

Bonus points for...

  • Masters in related field (International Relations, Military Strategy, Defense Studies)
  • Graduate of the United States Army Command and General Staff College (CGSC)
  • Experience within a US Special Operations Command (SOCOM) Unit
  • Experience deploying to the INDOPACOM AOR, with a deep understanding of the regional security landscape, operational challenges, and strategic goals
  • Language proficiency in any of the INDOPACOM regions
  • Language proficiency in the Chinese language (Mandarin or Cantonese) highly desirable
  • Clearance: Possess and maintain a Secret clearance 

Physical Requirements

May require working in an office, industrial, shipboard, or laboratory environment. Capable of climbing ladders and tolerating confined spaces and extreme temperature variances.


The listed salary range for this role is intended as a good faith estimate based on the role's location, expectations, and responsibilities. When extending an offer, HII's Mission Technologies division takes a variety of factors into consideration which include, but are not limited to, the role's function and a candidate's education or training, work experience, and key skills.

Our team of more than 7,000 professionals worldwide delivers all-domain expertise and advanced technologies in service of mission partners across the globe. Mission Technologies is leading the next evolution of national defense – the data evolution - by accelerating a breadth of national security solutions for government and commercial customers. Our capabilities range from C5ISR, AI and Big Data, cyber operations and synthetic training environments to fleet sustainment, environmental remediation and the largest family of unmanned underwater vehicles in every class. jobs.title: Change Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Job Description

The Change Manager is responsible for driving successful adoption of strategic, organizational, process, and technology changes across the commercial business. This role partners closely with senior leaders, program teams, and impacted stakeholders to ensure changes are well understood, effectively implemented, and sustained over time. 

The Change Manager brings structure, clarity, and empathy to complex change environments—balancing rigor in change planning with strong stakeholder engagement to minimize disruption and accelerate business outcomes.

Key Responsibilities 

Change Strategy & Planning 

  • Complete thorough stakeholder analysis for change initiatives 
  • Assess change impact across people, processes, systems, and culture 
  • Define change scope, readiness, risks, and success measures 
  • Develop and execute comprehensive change management strategies aligned to business objectives and program goals 
  • Integrate change milestones and plans with overall program/project plans and milestones 

Stakeholder & Leadership Engagement 

  • Lead alignment with senior stakeholders and project sponsors to define change success metrics. Monitor, measure and actively communicate metrics to stakeholders throughout change lifecycle. 
  • Identify and manage key stakeholder groups across global, regional, and functional teams 
  • Partner with leaders to clarify change rationale, desired behaviors, and expectations 
  • Equip leaders and managers with tools to role‑model and reinforce change 
  • Cultivate strong partnerships with change sponsors to ensure active, vocal advocacy of change to ensure adoption and benefit realization. 
  • Act as a trusted advisor to program and functional leadership on change risks and mitigation 

Communication & Enablement 

  • Design and execute targeted communication strategies tailored to different audiences 
  • Architect case for change for change initiatives, aligned to strategic priorities and overall change portfolio, including compelling messaging that explains the “why,” “what,” and “what’s changing for me” 
  • Coordinate closely with Communications and Enablement teams to ensure consistency and timing 
  • Support development of training, job aids, and adoption tools as needed 

Adoption, Readiness & Reinforcement 

  • Conduct change readiness assessments and track adoption metrics 
  • Identify potential resistance to change and develop change management strategies to proactively mitigate resistance and promote adoption 
  • Implement reinforcement strategies to embed new ways of working 
  • Monitor post‑implementation outcomes and recommend corrective actions 

Governance & Continuous Improvement 

  • Apply structured change methodologies and best practices 
  • Maintain change artifacts (impact assessments, stakeholder maps, communication plans) 
  • Capture lessons learned and continuously improve change capability across the organization 
  • Promote awareness and adoption of change management practices by educating stakeholders on its value, principles, and organizational impact, fostering a culture of change capability 

Required Qualifications 

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, HR, Organizational Development, Psychology, or related field (or equivalent experience) 
  • Proven experience leading change management for complex initiatives (e.g., transformation, technology, operating‑model change) 
  • Strong understanding of change management principles, tools, and methodologies 
  • Experience working in matrixed, global organizations 
  • Excellent communication, facilitation, and stakeholder‑management skills 
  • Ability to navigate ambiguity and influence without authority 

Preferred Qualifications 

  • Change management certification (e.g., Prosci, ACMP, or equivalent) 
  • 5-8 years experience supporting large‑scale commercial transformation or technology implementations (e.g., CRM, ERP, HRIS) 
  • Background in Transformation, PMO, Commercial or Strategy functions 
  • Data‑driven mindset with experience measuring adoption and change effectiveness 

Key Competencies 

  • Strategic thinking with practical execution mindset 
  • Strong business acumen and systems thinking 
  • High emotional intelligence and resilience 
  • Structured problem solving and risk management 
  • Collaborative, consultative leadership style 

Additional Information:

This role has a market-competitive salary with an anticipated base compensation of the following range: 83,700.00 - 100,000.00 USD. Actual salaries will vary depending on a candidate’s experience, qualifications, skills, and location. This role is eligible for a sales-based incentive or performance-based bonus. Other benefits include flexible working environment, comprehensive health insurance, industry-leading parental leave, life insurance, education support and more.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager — AI & Learner Experience
jobs.jd_full:
🌍 Location: Remote, Europe-based

About Nexford

We're building an AI-native university. Not a traditional LMS with an AI layer bolted on — something genuinely different, where AI is core to how learners experience education, stay engaged, and grow.

About the role

This is a senior B2C product role. You'll own parts of the learner journey end to end: from how prospective students discover us, through enrollment, through how they actually learn. What matters is whether learners come back not how many features shipped.

What we're looking for

  • You've built something recently — not managed a roadmap. A product, a feature, a side project. Something you can demo. Something real users touched. Something you iterated on based on what you saw.
  • You use AI tools daily and have shipped something with them in the last 3–6 months. You know the difference between a good use case and a gimmick.
  • You can hold a technical conversation with an engineer without needing a translator. Familarity with Cursor, Claude Code, Supabase, Vercel, etc.
  • You think in retention, conversion, engagement. You've moved one of these numbers before, and you can tell us by how much and why.
  • You work fast and clean. No process-around-process. You raise problems early, move through ambiguity, and leave things better than you found them.

What good looks like here

  • 5+ years in B2C product. Edtech, FinTech, subscriptions, consumer SaaS, or mission-driven consumer products.
  • A track record of moving retention, engagement, or conversion.
  • Something shipped with AI in the last 3–6 months both for work or personal project.
  • Comfortable going deep on systems, APIs, model trade-offs.
  • Bias toward simplicity and speed over process.
  • Have experience managing a team.
  • Edtech experience is a plus, not a requirement.

What you get

  • Competitive base + bonus
  • Generous holiday allowance
  • Discounted access to Nexford programs
  • A culture where opinions are expected and heard
  • Real ownership from day one
Salary: €70,000 – €95,000

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
jobs.jd_full:
WHO WE ARE:

Splice is a creative platform for people who make music. Serious producers choose Splice Sounds to bring their ideas to life. A subscription to Splice inspires and accelerates creative success for digital music creators with an industry-leading catalog of sounds and samples and an expanding AI stack. With a rent-to-own marketplace of DAWs and plugins, the Splice experience seamlessly integrates into any music production workflow, regardless of DAW (Digital Audio Workstation). Via Splice, an unparalleled team of sound designers and sample creators are fueling the success of a growing global community of chart-topping producers, students, and DIY creators.

HOW WE WORK:

At Splice, DISCO is a rallying cry for collaboration, accountability, and unity within our organization; Direct, Inclusive, Splice Together, Creator Centric, and Optimistic. Our shared success depends on our ability to support one another, work well together, and communicate directly. By embracing flexibility and a unified approach, we can navigate anything that’s thrown at us.

Splice embraces a culture of remote work. You’ll see your colleagues showing up from across the US and the UK. In order to keep us working well as a team, we have regular communication, including Town Halls, departmental all-hands and get-togethers.

When you join Splice, you join a network of colleagues, peers, and collaborators. Are you ready?

JOB TITLE: Senior Product Manager, Kits

LOCATION: US REMOTETHE ROLE:

We're looking for a senior, highly autonomous Product Manager to help grow and develop the Kits AI product line and business. Kits is Splice's AI vocal creation product: a tool that lets producers generate, transform, and create studio quality vocals with ease.

This is a rare opportunity: real ownership of growth and performance for a growing product, paired with the freedom to shape and launch entirely new product experiences alongside it. You'll identify high-impact opportunities, productize new ML capabilities into creator-facing workflows, and drive impact from day one.

You'll operate across both growth and product development (funnel optimization, pricing, monetization, and retention on one side; product strategy and new launches on the other), and you'll build a deep understanding of customer needs through research, analytics, and internal insight systems. Throughout, you'll use the latest AI tools to meaningfully increase your speed and leverage.

WHAT YOU’LL DO:

  • Own product growth and performance for Kits AI across funnel optimization, activation, retention, pricing, and monetization.
  • Drive Kits product strategy: identify high-impact opportunities and launch product and growth initiatives that move the business.
  • Productize new ML capabilities into valuable creator-facing products and workflows, partnering closely with research and engineering.
  • Develop a deep customer understanding through user interviews, analytics, and automated customer insight pipelines.
  • Track product performance in our data-analysis platform, own experimentation, and use data to make sharp prioritization calls.
  • Leverage the latest AI tools to dramatically accelerate your workflow, from shipping features directly to production with AI coding tools to building custom internal systems for customer insight, research, and product discovery.

JOB REQUIREMENTS:

  • 2–5 years of product management experience in a fast-moving, consumer or creator-facing environment, ideally a highly autonomous startup environment.
  • A track record of driving growth, product quality, or business performance through product work.
  • Comfort operating across strategy, execution, analytics, and customer research.
  • A metrics-informed mindset: fluent in funnel analysis, product analytics, and event tracking platforms like Amplitude.
  • Experience working on AI- or ML-powered products, or closely with research and engineering teams.
  • Strong judgment, high agency, and the ability to go from ambiguous opportunity to shipped product without waiting to be told.
  • Enthusiasm for using the latest AI tools to increase speed, leverage, and execution quality.

NICE TO HAVES:

  • Experience in music, creator tools, or the broader creative industry.
  • Familiarity with growth funnels, pricing and monetization experiments, or lifecycle engagement.
  • Exposure to AI-assisted workflows for research synthesis, spec writing, or internal tooling.

SPLICE BENEFITS:

  • Compensation & Equity: Competitive pay with annual reviews and equity opportunities.
  • Time Off: Unlimited PTO to recharge and thrive.
  • Health & Wellness: Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision coverage for you and your dependents.
  • Retirement Savings: 401(k) plan with immediate vesting and company match.
  • Parental Support: 12 weeks of fully paid parental leave for non-birthing parents, and 18-20 weeks for birthing parents.
  • Flexible Work Options: Work remotely or connect at our office hubs and creative spaces worldwide.
  • Professional Growth: Annual learning budget, leadership programs, and team ambassador opportunities.
  • Community & Connection: Inclusive events, team meet-ups, and vibrant Employee Resource Groups.

The national pay range for this role is $148,320 - $185,400. Individual compensation will be commensurate with the candidate's experience.

jobs.title: Product Manager (m/f/d)
jobs.jd_full:
Munich | Hybrid (3 days remote) | Full-time | Product

The role

As Product Manager, you work end-to-end: you understand customer needs, validate them early, prioritise according to business potential, and ship in tight feedback loops. You partner closely with Engineering, Design, and Go-to-Market so the solutions we build are useful in daily work, not just convincing in a demo.

At Alasco, you will build products that help real estate teams plan, control, and communicate financial decisions across investment and construction projects.

AI is part of how we build product at Alasco. In this role, you will also identify where AI can remove manual work, improve decision quality, or make complex workflows easier to handle for our customers.

What you will do

  • Evaluate product opportunities strategically: Identify and prioritize product opportunities based on customer workflows, product data and business value, and turn them into clear recommendations.
  • Validate fast and stay close to users: Run customer interviews, test assumptions early, and use feedback to sharpen the problem before the team builds.
  • Own features from discovery to rollout: Define scope, success criteria, and priorities, ship incrementally, and improve features based on usage and customer feedback.
  • Work closely with Engineering and Design: Shape solutions together, clarify edge cases, make trade-offs visible, and help the team move from concept to production.
  • Turn market feedback into product iterations: Discover customer needs, product signals, and analyse Go-to-Market feedback, to translate them into concrete product investments.
  • Contribute to AI-supported product work: Identify use cases where AI can solve a clear customer problem, and help define quality expectations, evaluation criteria, feedback loops, and user-facing guidance.
  • Align stakeholders around decisions: Keep Product, Design, Engineering, Management, and Go-to-Market aligned by making priorities, risks, and trade-offs explicit.

What we’re looking for

  • Deep experience building B2B SaaS products end-to-end, from discovery to launch and iteration.
  • Strong product judgment in prioritization, assumption mapping, success metrics, and decisions with incomplete information.
  • Comfort working closely with engineers and designers, including shaping solutions, clarifying edge cases, and moving from concept to production.
  • A practical understanding of software products, data-rich workflows, and the constraints that affect product decisions.
  • Experience with AI in product development, with enough hands-on curiosity to understand where it helps, where it fails, and what users need to trust it.
  • Clear communication in German and English.

Nice to have

  • Experience with B2B software in regulated or complex domains (e.g., finance, construction, real estate).
  • You have worked with evaluation setups, prompting strategies, or human-in-the-loop workflows.

What we offer

  • VSOP: you participate in Alasco’s long-term success and share in the upside you help create.
  • €2,000 learning and development budget per year plus 3 L&D days.
  • Hybrid setup: 2 days in our Munich office, 3 days remote.
  • Up to 4 weeks workation per year.
  • 28 days vacation.
  • Navit or Wellpass subsidy of €40 per month.
  • Regular company and team events.
  • Modern tools, including AI, to help you work more efficiently, stay better prepared, and learn faster.
  • A product problem space with real complexity: large budgets, many stakeholders, and decisions that matter long before a building is finished.

Hiring process

  • Intro Call with Recruiting
  • Hiring Manager Interview
  • On-site Interview (including a task)
  • Meet one of the Co-CEOs

About us

Real estate is one of the world’s largest, most capital-intensive - and least digitized - industries. We’re changing that. Alasco is building the leading AI Platform for Real Estate, purpose-built for an era in which AI fundamentally reshapes how decisions are made. If you want to work where technological possibility has a direct seat at the decision-making table, this is your place.

We deliver impact to several hundred customers already today - making investment and construction decisions measurably better for our customers. And we’re just getting started.

We welcome applications from all people. Regardless of gender, origin, religion, age, sexual orientation, or disability.

jobs.title: Product Manager, Billing
jobs.jd_full:
Highlights

  • Location: Poland, Remote (CET)
  • Language:Russian-speaking team; English fluency required (C1)

About Fundraise Up

We’re Fundraise Up - a global fundraising platform built to make donating to nonprofits fast, seamless, and accessible to all. Every month, our technology powers tens of millions of dollars in donations across the globe. We focus on innovation that directly impacts results: faster load times, higher conversion rates, global payment support, and accessibility-first design.

Our platform is trusted by many of the world’s leading nonprofits, including UNICEF, the Alzheimer’s Association, and a wide range of global NGOs. With a 4.9⁄5 rating across top software review platforms, we’re recognized not just for our impact - but for the quality of the product we deliver.

A Truly Global Product

We operate in the enterprise segment, serving nonprofit organizations across North America, the United Kingdom, Australia, and Europe.

We’re building a large and complex product ecosystem that serves nonprofits, donors, and partners around the world. The platform includes a modern checkout experience and customizable widgets(each a standalone SPA), donor, organization, and partner portals, admin tools, and several internal apps.

The Team

We are a distributed team of 160+ product professionals. Our team members are mainly based across Spain, Poland, Portugal, Georgia, Armenia, Serbia, Turkey, and Cyprus.

Despite our scale, we operate like a focused team - where every task matters and every voice is heard. We value thoughtful collaboration, strong engineering practices, and a product mindset. You’ll be joining a team where quality, mentorship, and mutual respect come first.

About the Role

We are looking for a Product Manager, Billing own and evolve Fundraise Up’s Billing domain end-to-end: transaction-based fee handling, fee coverage, payouts, refunds and the financial flows behind donations. We charge organizations a percentage fee per transaction (netted from payouts), which donors can optionally cover.

Core Mission & Priorities

  • Reliability first: keep donation money flow stable, correct, and predictable (uptime + accuracy).
  • Invisible billing for orgs: minimize billing friction so organizations rarely have to think about payments engine.
  • Growth enabler: unlock expansion via payment method coverage, localized compliance needs, retries engine and new verticals readiness.

What You’ll Do

  • Own the Billing product area roadmap, outcomes, and prioritization; define quarterly goals and success metrics.
  • Partner with Engineering to deliver resilient billing systems (accuracy, idempotency, retries, monitoring, incident response, and clear ownership boundaries).
  • Improve revenue protection and customer experience via dunning, failed payment recovery, refunds, and dispute/chargeback-related flows (as applicable).
  • Define and maintain billing domain documentation: data model, event flows, edge cases, and operational run-books.
  • Run discovery with internal teams (GTM & Product Analyst) and customers to uncover pain points and opportunities; turn insights into clear PRDs and delivery plans.
  • Establish and track core Billing KPIs (e.g., failed payment rate, recovery rate).
  • Drive antifraud strategy execution: define priorities and success metrics; partner with Eng/Data to ship realtime protections, monitoring/alerting, and fraud-response capabilities, and continuously improve rules based on observed patterns.

Requirements

  • 4–7 years of product management experience with at least 1–2 years owning a technically complex domain.
  • Strong understanding of payment processing, payouts, fee models, refunds/chargebacks, multi-processor setups, and reconciliation.
  • Proven ability to work with engineers on backend/system design trade-offs and to ship reliable platform capabilities.
  • Comfort with data: defining metrics, building a measurement plan, and using analysis to prioritize and evaluate impact.
  • Clear written communication and structured thinking; can turn ambiguity into executable plans.
  • High ownership, high discipline, and strong cross-functional collaboration.

Nice To Have

  • Experience with payment processors (Stripe Payments, PayPal, etc.) and/or financial systems integrations.
  • Experience in B2B SaaS pricing & packaging or self-serve account management.
  • Familiarity with audit/compliance requirements impacting billing (SOX readiness, SOC2 implications, etc.).
  • Experience reducing billing-related support contacts through product + tooling.

Why work with us

  • A strong, collaborative product team that owns what it builds
  • Clear product vision and access to real customer feedback from global nonprofit leaders
  • Flat structure: no politics, just great work with great people
  • Transparent company culture-we share how we’re growing, where revenue comes from, and what’s next
  • Long-term focus: we offer equity options and value sustained, meaningful contribution

Compensation

  • The compensation range for this role is €5,800 - €6,300 gross.
  • Final compensation will be determined based on relevant experience, skills, qualifications, alignment with the role’s requirements and location.

Benefits

  • 31 days off
  • 100% paid telemedicine plan
  • Home Office Setup Assistance: the company offers assistance with purchasing furniture (office chair, office desk, monitor) and other items to create a comfortable workspace.
  • English learning courses (50% reimbursement, up to $1,000 annually).
  • Relevant professional education (100% reimbursement, up to $1,500 annually).
  • Gym or swimming pool (50% reimbursement, up to $500 annually).
  • Co-working (up to $250 monthly).
  • Remote working.

We are an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national origin, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law in the countries where we operate.

We are looking for a (Senior) Product Manager to own and drive Immowelt’s Core Classified and Import Products — a suite of platform-enablement products that sit at the heart of how real estate data flows into and out of our marketplace.

You will own the full product lifecycle across data import infrastructure, classified listing products, and data-as-a-service exports: from defining the vision and roadmap to driving measurable delivery with engineering, data, and commercial teams.

You will report directly to the Head of Product and work closely with cross-functional squads across Immowelt and the Aviv Group. We’re looking for a strong Senior PM or a mid-level PM ready to grow into real ownership and broader scope.

WHAT YOU WILL DO

  • Set and execute the product strategy across your portfolio. Own the vision, mission, roadmap, backlog, and delivery — aligned with Immowelt’s OKRs and business targets.
  • Lead high-impact platform enablement and transformation initiatives, partnering closely with engineering, data, ops, and business stakeholders across the organisation.

Product Discovery & Strategy

  • Research and synthesise market signals, B2B customer needs, operational pain points, and technical constraints into a prioritised roadmap.
  • Define measurable goals, success KPIs, and north-star metrics for all initiatives — and track them rigorously.

Delivery & Execution

  • Drive end-to-end product delivery in close partnership with engineering, design, and data teams.

  • Manage planning, prioritisation, dependencies, and risks across multiple simultaneous workstreams.
  • Lead platform modernisation efforts — including the migration from legacy on-premise infrastructure to cloud — with clear migration plans and stakeholder communication.
  • Ensure every impact of your delivery on other teams is communicated, aligned, and transparent.

Stakeholder Communication & Alignment

  • Align stakeholders — from engineering to senior leadership — on strategy, priorities, and progress with full transparency.

  • Present roadmap updates and delivery outcomes to leadership using strategic context and clear storytelling.
  • Coordinate across teams for dependency management, synchronisation, and joint decision-making.

Data & Analytics

  • Track and report on product KPIs to measure success, identify failures, and drive continuous improvement.
  • Use data and analytics to deeply understand the current state, define the target state, and identify the best path of transformation.
  • Define instrumentation requirements to ensure full measurability of product outcomes.

Qualifications

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR

Experience & Background

  • Several years of product management experience, or close and hands-on collaboration with product managers in a software product environment.
  • Proven ability to move quickly between big-picture strategy and technical implementation details.
  • Deep understanding of how high-velocity software development teams work and how engineers think.
  • Experience with platform enablement, API and integration products, Partnership ecosystem creation and data services.
  • Background in transformation projects — where both tech/infrastructure and business enablement products are being modernised simultaneously.

  • Technical & Analytical Skills
  • Strong analytical capabilities — you understand current challenges deeply, define the best target state, and map the transformation journey clearly.
  • Technical fluency to engage meaningfully in architecture discussions, API design, and build vs. buy decisions.
  • Solid grasp of integration patterns, microservices, event-driven systems, and cloud infrastructure concepts.

Communication & Mindset

We’re creating first-of-its-kind technology that will transform transportation. To do so successfully, we must design for everyone in our cities and on our roads. We believe in building a great place to work through a progressive, global culture that is diverse, inclusive, and ensures people feel valued at every level of the organization. Diversity helps us to see the world differently; it’s not only good for our business, it’s the right thing to do.

Scale up, not starting up.

Our team is behind some of the industry’s largest leaps forward, including the first fully-autonomous cross-country drive in the U.S, the launch of the world’s first robotaxi pilot, and operation of the world’s longest-standing public robotaxi fleet. We’re driven to scale; we’re moving towards commercialization of our technology, and we need team members who are ready to embrace change and challenges.

Formed as a joint venture between Hyundai Motor Group and Aptiv, Motional is fundamentally changing how people move through their lives. Headquartered in Boston, Motional has operations in the U.S and Asia. For more information, visit  www.Motional.com and follow us on Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram and YouTube.

Motional AD Inc. is an EOE. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. To comply with Federal Law, we participate in E-Verify. All newly-hired employees are queried through this electronic system established by the DHS and the SSA to verify their identity and employment eligibility.

jobs.title: Delivery Manager
jobs.jd_full:
Delivery Manager

Location: Budapest, Hungary

Hybrid Working - 2 Days in the office

Salary: 1.5m - 2.2m HUF per month

Hi, I’m Zoe Evans, Senior Delivery Manager at Hawk-Eye Innovations in the Tracking and Auto Officiating Value Stream, and we’re looking for a Delivery Manager to join our growing technology unit.

This is an exciting opportunity to join one of the core pillars of Hawk-Eye Innovations. Since our inception in 2001, we have been a leading provider of advanced officiating and tracking tools for elite sports. Our team spearheads the creation of sophisticated optical tracking systems, managing an end-to-end remit that encompasses everything from initial camera capture to the delivery of real-time data.

You will be joining a collaborative, cross-functional Delivery Team that partners directly with Product, Software Engineering, and Operations. Unlike traditional delivery roles tied to a single silo, you will orchestrate the seamless execution of diverse initiatives across multiple squads and solution teams, helping us provide both integrated services and fully automated officiating technology to the biggest names in the global sports industry.

About the Role

We’re looking for someone who enjoys bringing people together, solving complex problems, and helping highly skilled technical teams deliver their best work.

As a Delivery Manager, you will underpin the timing, flow, and execution coordination within our value stream. Working alongside Product Managers, Engineering Leads, and Operations, you will lead multiple projects that accomplish key company objectives, whether that’s launching a new product, integrating existing offerings into new markets, or improving our core technical foundations for future success.

This isn’t a traditional, rigid project management role. We operate in a fast-paced, reactive environment where priorities can shift as new opportunities emerge. We need someone who can quickly assess impacts, manage uncertainty, and build capability in a growing business, all while ensuring our teams have the environment they need to thrive.

What You’ll Work On

  • Leading project delivery across multiple tracking and automated officiating initiatives to tight deadlines.

  • Coordinating work cross-functionally across multiple engineering squads, product teams, and operational units.

  • Managing delivery roadmaps, timelines, milestones, and project planning activities.

  • Driving agile maturity and coaching best practices within the value stream to foster a continuous delivery culture.

  • Tracking and communicating timelines to the wider business, ensuring stakeholders have clear visibility of project status.

  • Identifying dependency collisions, delivery risks, and sequencing issues early to implement mitigation measures.

  • Utilizing data to your advantage by measuring the success of outcomes and identifying areas for process optimization.

  • Building key relationships both internally and externally to facilitate organizational change and transformation.

What Success Looks Like

Your first few months will focus on learning our sophisticated optical tracking systems, understanding how our cross-functional squads interact, and building strong relationships across the business.

As your confidence grows, you will become the go-to person for implementing and communicating project best practices throughout the product lifecycle, from idea to execution. Supported by the Head of Delivery and VS Delivery Leads, you will take on increasingly complex projects and play an active role in the leadership of the wider value stream.

Success in this role means establishing yourself as a trusted partner who anticipates emerging issues ahead of time, keeps work moving efficiently, and empowers our technical teams to deliver world-class sports technology.

Key Responsibilities

  • Coordinate the successful delivery of multiple cross-functional projects aligned with company priorities.

  • Maintain and continuously evolve team roadmaps, milestones, and project timelines.

  • Drive agile and scrum practices, tailoring the project management methodology to fit different scenarios.

  • Track and manage project dependencies, risks, and blockers, establishing robust contingency plans.

  • Support senior stakeholder engagement and provide regular, data-driven progress reporting.

  • Identify areas for process optimization and implement improvements that enhance delivery efficiency.

  • Foster a collaborative, empowering project environment that allows technical teams to do their best work.

  • Actively contribute to the collective Delivery Team, sharing best practices and supporting the growth of the value stream.

Skills, Knowledge & Experience

We’re looking for someone with:

  • Proven experience delivering and leading cross-functional projects based on company priorities to tight deadlines.

  • Comfort throughout all aspects of the project lifecycle, including the effective management of artifacts and tracking tools.

  • Strong understanding of various project management methodologies, including Agile and Scrum, with the ability to implement the right approach for the scenario.

  • Excellent communication and relationship-building skills, with experience in senior stakeholder engagement.

  • Ability to understand complex systems in order to anticipate, surface, and mitigate emerging issues ahead of time.

  • Comfort in a reactive, fast-paced environment, with the ability to quickly assess impacts and propose informed paths forward.

  • A data-driven mindset, utilizing metrics to offer process improvements or measure the success of outcomes.

  • Experience managing uncertainty and spotting/building team capability within a growing business.

Why Join Us?

This is an opportunity to join a business at the absolute forefront of sports technology, working alongside the foremost technical minds in the industry.

You will interact with some of the biggest brands in elite sports, working on disruptive technology that directly impacts major sporting events worldwide. As part of a highly collaborative delivery community, you will receive dedicated support from senior delivery leadership to continuously develop your skills, broaden your exposure to complex projects, and meaningfully shape the future of sports officiating.

Reward, Benefits & Wellness

In addition to being a valued member of our team and working in an engaging, creative environment, we offer:

  • Annual Leave: Generous annual leave in line with Hungarian local labor law.

  • Flexibility: Hybrid working model with 2 days per week in the office to support your work-life balance.

  • Health & Wellbeing: Private medical insurance, life & accident insurance, MindentMent Dental Clinic discounts, and access to an Employee Assistance Program.

  • Lifestyle & Perks: Access to sporting events and tickets, plus exclusive Sony Group Company discounts.

  • Cafeteria Benefits: A flexible choice of perks including a SZÉP Card, Nursery & Kindergarten contributions, and an annual MOL Bubi Pass.

  • Rewards: Seniority awards to celebrate your long-term dedication.

Equal Opportunity & Inclusion

Hawk-Eye Innovations is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment. We believe that passion for innovation thrives when different perspectives come together. We employ, retain, promote, and treat all employees and applicants fairly based on their skills, qualifications, and experience, ensuring a respectful environment where everyone can succeed.

jobs.title: Senior Project Manager - US Remote
jobs.jd_full:
What is PerfectServe?

PerfectServe is a leading provider of clinical communication and physician scheduling solutions in the health IT space. The company was founded in 1997 and has grown steadily since then, with a notable jump after several major acquisitions were announced in 2019. PerfectServe now has 400+ employees, 30,000+ customers — spanning medical practices, hospitals, and health systems — and $100 million+ in annual revenue.

PerfectServe’s mission is to accelerate speed to care by optimizing provider schedules and routing communications — including messages, pages, calls, and alerts — to the right place at the right time in any care setting. By facilitating real-time information sharing, building better schedules, and automating important clinical workflows, we believe we can help our customers advance patient care and improve the well-being of their clinicians.

Leading analyst firms like Gartner® and KLAS Research have consistently validated our approach:

  1. In 2026, PerfectServe was named highest in execution and furthest in vision in the Gartner Magic Quadrant™ for Clinical Communication and Collaboration — the clear segment leader.
  2. PerfectServe also received two Best in KLAS awards in 2026 — one for physician scheduling and another for ambulatory clinical communications. That makes for 11 Best in KLAS awards over the past 9 years.

At PerfectServe, you’ll have a unique opportunity to join a collaborative team with decades of experience that finds new ways to delight our customers every day. This involves consistent efforts to stay on the cutting edge of product development, which includes everything from implementing AI in new and existing solutions to brainstorming with customers about novel workflows. But you don’t have to be in product to make in impact — everybody at PerfectServe contributes to important work that moves the business forward.

If you’re looking for a well-established, tech-forward company full of smart people doing meaningful work, you’ve found the right place!

At PerfectServe, we believe better communication drives better healthcare. Every interaction across the care continuum — between clinicians, patients, operational teams, and support staff — depends on timely, intelligent coordination. Our mission is to simplify that complexity through technology that helps healthcare organizations communicate more effectively, operate more efficiently, and deliver better experiences across the continuum of care.

PerfectServe partners with health systems, physician practices, and healthcare organizations across the country to improve clinical communication, scheduling, patient engagement, and operational workflows. We are recognized as a leader in healthcare communication and collaboration because we combine innovation, operational rigor, and deep healthcare expertise with a relentless focus on customer experience.

The Opportunity

You are the connective tissue. No direct reports, no channels, no content — and yet you may be the reason the whole engine keeps time. You own the operating rhythm, the intake that keeps content and design from being pulled apart, the systems the team runs on, and the rollout of AI workflows across marketing. Most of the overlaps in this org get resolved at the seams, and you own the seams.

This is a high-trust, low-ego role for someone who would rather make a team faster than take the credit. With a sale on an 18-month horizon, on-time delivery and a clean operating story are not nice-to-haves.

What You’ll Own

Operating rhythm

  • Own the planning cadence, QBRs, roadmap, and the campaign and content intake and traffic system.
  • Manage dependencies across pillars so the creation-to-conversion handoff and the content queue actually move.

Systems and marketing operations

  • Own the martech and process backbone: Salesforce and Pardot administration in partnership with RevOps (only owning the marketing specific workflows)
  • Own the attribution model’s plumbing so the pipeline story is clean enough for a board or a data room.

AI enablement

  • Support the rollout of AI and agentic workflows across the team; define the playbooks, drive adoption, and track hours saved.
  • Identify opportunities for leveraging AI for scale.

Initiative delivery

  • Run delivery for the big cross-functional programs: the AI-first repositioning and exit readiness.

What You Don’t Own

  • Channel strategy, content, or creative — those are the pillars; you make their work land on time.
  • Any pipeline number — you’re measured on delivery and adoption, not demand.

Key Performance Indicators

  • On-time delivery. Cross-functional initiatives delivered on schedule.
  • Cycle time. Brief-to-launch time, shortening over time.
  • AI adoption. Share of the team using defined AI workflows, and hours saved.
  • Planning hygiene. Cadence adherence and dependency clarity across pillars.
  • Milestone delivery. Repositioning and exit-readiness milestones hit on schedule.

What We’re Looking For

  • 6+ years in program or project management, marketing operations, or revenue operations, ideally in B2B SaaS.
  • A builder of operating systems and cadences, not just a tracker of tasks.
  • Hands-on with Salesforce, Pardot/Marketo, and project tooling.
  • An AI-first operator who has rolled out agentic workflows and driven real adoption.
  • Calm, organized, and able to influence without authority.

Nice to Have

  • Experience standing up a marketing operations function.
  • Experience supporting a company through diligence or an exit.

Why Join PerfectServe?

At PerfectServe, we are transforming healthcare communication and collaboration to help clinicians deliver better care. You’ll work with a dedicated and mission‑driven team in an environment that values growth, transparency, and innovation.

We offer a salary range of $115,000 USD per year, with compensation tailored to your background, strengths, and growth potential. This range reflects our commitment to pay transparency and is based on market data, internal equity, and the scope of responsibilities. Compensation is determined by several factors, including experience, skills, and team impact. We regularly review compensation to ensure fairness and consistency. Current employees with questions are encouraged to connect with their manager or People Operations.

Benefits:

  • Remote first work environment
  • Health, Dental, Vision, Life and Disability Insurance options available day one.
  • 401K - with match and immediately vested.
  • 17 company holidays, 2 floating holidays plus competitive paid time off policy
  • Internal Advancement Opportunities

PerfectServe offers unified healthcare communication solutions to help physicians, nurses, and care team members provide exceptional patient care. PerfectServe’s cloud-based solutions enhance patient safety and reduce provider burnout by automating workflows, speeding time to treatment, optimizing shift schedules, empowering nurse mobility, and engaging patients in their own care.

jobs.jd_full:
ABOUT HALTER

At Halter, we’re on a mission to enable farmers and graziers to run the most productive and sustainable operations. Our customers are using Halter to break free from the time-intensive constraints of conventional practices. Imagine watching 500 cattle stand up and walk calmly towards their next break? No quad bikes, no dogs, no fences. Just a group of cattle walking at their own pace. People say it looks like magic. Our customers are revolutionizing grazing with Halter. It's changing lives and transforming an industry. People join Halter to do meaningful work. By joining us you’ll be solving challenging problems within a talented team and a culture built for high performance. Our team out-think, out-work and out-care. We’re committed to delivering real change in the world - this isn’t easy, and in truth, we love that it’s hard.

We’re backed to deliver on a mission that matters by Tier 1 investors including Founders Fund https://foundersfund.com, Bessemer Venture Partners https://www.bvp.com/, BOND, https://www.bondcap.com/ DCVC https://www.dcvc.com/, Blackbird https://www.blackbird.vc/, Promus Ventures https://www.promusventures.com/, Rocket Lab’s Peter Beck and Icehouse ventures https://www.icehouseventures.co.nz/?utm_term=icehouse%20ventures&utm_campaign=Brand+Keywords&utm_source=adwords&utm_medium=ppc&hsa_acc=4064636360&hsa_cam=16902737884&hsa_grp=134004309485&hsa_ad=592971340272&hsa_src=g&hsa_tgt=kwd-1082530126230&hsa_kw=icehouse%20ventures&hsa_mt=b&hsa_net=adwords&hsa_ver=3&gclid=Cj0KCQjw2v-gBhC1ARIsAOQdKY0J0DepRVFDmjQlAkJPbZuQnLSA5UVzUzOXYiPxkjX-SeVa513_BhkaAr-MEALw_wcB.

To find out more, visit our LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/halter-limited & Instagram https://www.instagram.com/lifeathalter/.




ABOUT THE ROLE:

We’re looking for a Product Manager to join our Virtual Fencing team.

Virtual Fencing is the heart of Halter. It’s how thousands of farmers across the world manage their animals. Halter is often farmers #1 app on their phone and we have the privilege of building the experience that farmers use to look after their animals.

This role is all about deeply understanding what our farmers need from our product and taking those insights and converting them into beautiful elegant features that help our farmers run their farm. This role sits right at the intersection of technology and farming. You will become an expert in virtual fencing; developing a deep understanding of how the technology works and how farmers and ranchers use it in practice. You’ll work closely with our farmers and the rest of the product team to build an epic product that changes lives.

You don’t necessarily need any Product Management experience but it’s crucial you understand farming. If you’re curious, hungry to learn, love solving hard problems and are passionate about agriculture, let’s talk!

 


WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - You’ve worked on farm as a shepherd, farm hand, farm manager or as a Rural Professional so aren’t new to the realities of farming and you’re ready to take that experience into a role that directly helps farmers.

 - You’re highly curious and hungry to become an expert in Virtual Fencing and understand how the tech really works.

 - You love working at a fast pace with loose requirements. You’re confident navigating uncertainty, breaking down messy problems, testing ideas, pivoting and getting stuck in.

 - You’re a critical thinker who is able to break down complex problems. Even when the pressure’s on, you can get to simple solutions.

 - You can clearly and simply communicate concepts or decisions to engineers, farmers and other Halter teams.

 - You care about simplicity, usability and elegance. You sweat the details because you know they matter, especially for complex products used in demanding, real-world environments.




WHAT YOUR DAY COULD LOOK LIKE:

 - You will get out on farm regularly, gumboots on, talking to farmers about their operation, understanding how Halter fits into their day.

 - Work out what farmers truly need (not just what they're asking for) and how it should work in a real dairy or beef environment.

 - Help decide what gets built next and why, prioritising based on what'll make the biggest difference on farm.

 - Work closely with other Product Managers, Engineers and Designers to get features built and shipped.

 - Make sure new features actually land well for farmers, from rollout through to adoption





OUR BENEFITS:

 - Delicious snacks and drinks are available for your daily flow.

 - Healthy body, healthy mind. We're partnered with Southern Cross Health Insurance to support your well-being.

 - We offer 6 months of fully paid parental leave for primary caregivers, 4 weeks of fully paid secondary caregiver leave and many other parental benefits that support you and your family.

 - Our personal growth is important. Halter offers an annual $1000 self-development budget to be used for anything that fuels personal growth.

 - Our time to recharge is valued, we're offered wellness leave and unlimited paid annual leave.

 - Importantly, we offer an inclusive and attractive remuneration package made up of salary, benefits and an employee stock ownership plan.




OUR OFFICE FIRST APPROACH

Humans are wired for connection. Being office first is a core pillar of our culture because in-person connections drive your growth, learning, impact, and relationships. Strong relationships make it easier to disagree, give feedback, and do meaningful work together.

We're office first, not office only. Working from the office is our default, but we flex when needed. We have a high-trust culture where everyone is trusted to do what's best for Halter.

Our office vibe is special, the high energy and spectacular people make work enjoyable. Your growth and impact are unlimited here, coming from solving problems, innovating, and constantly learning from each other.




JOIN OUR TEAM

Halter is committed to promoting a diverse and inclusive workplace — a place where we can each be ourselves and do the best work of our lives. Research shows that while men apply to jobs when they meet an average of 60% of the requirements, women and under-represented groups of candidates tend to only apply when they meet every requirement. If you think you have what it takes but don’t necessarily tick every requirement on this job description, please still get in touch and apply to Halter. We’d love to chat to see if you’ll be an epic fit!

If this opportunity sounds like you, please apply below by sending through your cover letter explaining why you’re excited about this role and working at Halter, along with your CV, and we’ll be in touch!

Please also feel free to check out the careers page https://www.halterhq.com/careers for more information on working at Halter and don't forget to follow us on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/halter-limited & Instagram https://www.instagram.com/lifeathalter/.




WHY OUR TEAM LOVES WORKING AT HALTER:

 - Work that genuinely matters. Every now and again a company comes along that transforms an entire industry and leaves the world in a better place. Our team gets to be part of something truly meaningful, helping farmers improve their livelihoods, spend more time with their families, and build more sustainable operations.

 - Spectacular people solving hard problems. Our culture is designed for talented people to do work that changes lives. The team is filled with diverse, kind, and driven people who push each other to do their best work. You'll be thrown into the deep end, tackling complex challenges and building something tangible that solves real problems.

 - You'll grow here. Autonomy, mastery, and learning define how we work. You'll have the freedom to work on interesting problems, master new skills, and continuously develop yourself, both through your role and our $1,000 personal growth fund.

 - This isn't easy, and we love that it's hard. Working at Halter will be the most rewarding and the most challenging work of your life. We move fast, take bold bets, and work hard to reshape an entire industry. As one team member put it: "Joining Halter is a bit like strapping yourself to a rocket ship, but it's an epic journey to be a part of!"
